Musical artist

**Wayne Ford Levy** (born c.1981),[[1]](#cite_note-Krewen-1) known by his stage name **Exco Levi**, is a [Brampton](/wiki/Brampton "Brampton")-based, Jamaican-Canadian [reggae](/wiki/Reggae "Reggae") musician.[[2]](#cite_note-Patrick-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-Sticking-4) Levi has won five [Juno Awards](/wiki/Juno_Award "Juno Award").[[5]](#cite_note-lfpress.com-5)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Exco_Levi&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Born in [Harmons](/w/index.php?title=Harmons,_Jamaica&action=edit&redlink=1 "Harmons, Jamaica (page does not exist)") in [Manchester Parish](/wiki/Manchester_Parish "Manchester Parish"), Jamaica, Levi's early singing experience came from the school choir.[[1]](#cite_note-Krewen-1) His father, Michael 'Mr Cool' Levy worked as a selector on the Super Soul [sound system](/wiki/Sound_system_(Jamaican) "Sound system (Jamaican)") in [Clarendon](/wiki/Clarendon_Parish,_Jamaica "Clarendon Parish, Jamaica").[[6]](#cite_note-6) He moved to Canada in 2005, and started his recording career in 2007, releasing the single "O' Canada" that year.[[7]](#cite_note-Panjwani-7) His 'Exco' stage name is a reference to his football idol, [Andrés Escobar](/wiki/Andr%C3%A9s_Escobar "Andrés Escobar").[[7]](#cite_note-Panjwani-7)

Levi was introduced to producer [Donovan Germain](/wiki/Donovan_Germain "Donovan Germain") by [Richie Stephens](/wiki/Richie_Stephens_(musician) "Richie Stephens (musician)"), and the two began working together.[[4]](#cite_note-Sticking-4) His track "Bleaching Shop", recorded with Germain, won the [Juno Award](/wiki/Juno_Award "Juno Award") for [Reggae Recording of the Year](/wiki/Juno_Award_for_Reggae_Recording_of_the_Year "Juno Award for Reggae Recording of the Year") in 2012.[[8]](#cite_note-8) He also won Junos for "Storms of Life" (2013), and "Strive" (2014).[[9]](#cite_note-Obs1402-9) He has also worked with German producers Silly Walks.[[4]](#cite_note-Sticking-4)

In December 2014 he performed at Sting, and in January 2015 he was on the bill of [Rebel Salute](/wiki/Rebel_Salute "Rebel Salute").[[4]](#cite_note-Sticking-4) In March 2015 he released his second album, *Country Man*.[[10]](#cite_note-10) He won a fourth Juno Award for 2015's "Welcome the King".[[4]](#cite_note-Sticking-4)[[11]](#cite_note-11)

In 2016, Levi was living and maintaining a studio in [Brampton](/wiki/Brampton "Brampton"), Ontario, while also working at times in Jamaica. That year he was inducted into the [Brampton Arts Walk of Fame](/wiki/Brampton_Arts_Walk_of_Fame "Brampton Arts Walk of Fame").[[7]](#cite_note-Panjwani-7)

In 2017, Levi won a fifth Juno Award for his reggae song "Siren".[[5]](#cite_note-lfpress.com-5)[[12]](#cite_note-12) In November 2017, Levi released his third album, *Narrative*, featuring guest contributions from [Raging Fyah](/wiki/Raging_Fyah "Raging Fyah") and [Sizzla](/wiki/Sizzla "Sizzla").[[2]](#cite_note-Patrick-2)

In 2019, Levi was nominated for a Juno Award for the seventh time in eight years for his *Narrative* album[[13]](#cite_note-13) which was produced by Silly Walks from [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany").

In 2020, the song "Wah Gwaan" earned Levi his eighth Juno nomination.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

Most recently Levi received his ninth Juno Award nomination[[15]](#cite_note-15) in 2022, for the song "By Any Means".

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Exco_Levi&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Discography")]

### Albums

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Exco_Levi&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Albums")]

* *Words of the Wise* EP (2014), [VP](/wiki/VP_Records "VP Records")
* *Country Man* (2015), [High Priest](/wiki/High_Priest "High Priest")/[Penthouse](/wiki/Penthouse_Records "Penthouse Records")
* *Narrative* (2017), Silly Walks Discotheque
* *Black Creek* EP (2021), High Priest.
